discussion: Running in 'llm' mode, ReAct (single attempt) reached exact-match accuracy EM=0.7417 while Reflexion (up to several attempts with self-reflection) reached EM=0.95; accuracy improved by +0.2083 absolute. The gain comes at a cost: Reflexion averaged 1.325 attempts versus 1 for ReAct, spending about 815.16 more tokens and 1224.97 ms more latency per question on average. The most common ReAct failure modes were incomplete_multi_hop (17), wrong_final_answer (10), entity_drift (4), while Reflexion's residual failures were wrong_final_answer (3), incomplete_multi_hop (2), entity_drift (1). Reflection memory helped most when the first attempt stopped after the first hop or drifted to a distractor entity, because the reflector could name the missing hop and the actor could complete it next time. It helped least when the answer was simply absent from the provided context, where extra attempts only added cost. The key trade-off is accuracy versus token and latency budget, and evaluator quality bounds the measured gains.
